outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: 'We never asked for a ceasefire,' says Iran's foreign minister, as war keeps raging
outlets.summary: Araghchi rejects Trump's claim that Iran asked for negotiations. Six more US soldiers killed bringing total to 13.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Two weeks in, Trump has been knocked back on his political heels
outlets.summary: Analysis of Trump's weakening political position as the Iran war enters its third week. Democrats seize on oil prices and casualty numbers ahead of midterms.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: If Trump has already won the Iran war, why does he need foreign ships to help him end it?
outlets.summary: Analysis questions Trump's mixed messaging — claiming military victory while demanding allied warships join a coalition no one wants to join.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: US lifts Russian oil sanctions as Iran war pushes energy crisis to breaking point
outlets.summary: Frames Russia sanctions waiver as geopolitical failure — war forcing US to facilitate Russian oil trade. Covers Lebanon bridge destruction and mounting civilian toll across region.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump says he won't declare Iran war over after claiming US 'decimated' its military
outlets.summary: Trump claims Iran's air force and air defenses destroyed but refuses to declare victory. Energy Secretary predicts war ends in 'weeks.'

outlets.name: The Hill
outlets.headline: Trump announces devastating Kharg Island bombing raid — calls it 'most powerful in Middle East history'
outlets.summary: Trump claims Kharg Island military targets obliterated. Hegseth declares Iran's military destroyed and combat ineffective. Oil infrastructure deliberately spared.

outlets.name: Daily Wire
outlets.headline: Hegseth: Iran's missile and drone capacity nearly eliminated — 'core mission' is nuclear program
outlets.summary: Supportive framing of Pentagon briefing. Marines deployment as show of strength. Focus on nuclear program as justification.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war: What is happening on day 17 of US-Israel attacks?
outlets.summary: Day 17 roundup: Jerusalem hit by missile fragments near holy sites, Dubai airport drone strike, allies reject Hormuz coalition, Araghchi rejects ceasefire.

outlets.summary: Comprehensive roundup of Day 17 developments including allied rejection of Hormuz patrol and Jerusalem missile debris.

outlets.name: CNBC
outlets.headline: Iran vows to kill Israel's Netanyahu as impact of war on Gulf region widens
outlets.summary: Iran escalates rhetoric with direct threats against Netanyahu. War's economic impact spreading across Gulf region as Hormuz remains closed.

outlets.name: France 24
outlets.headline: Iran threatens retaliation as Trump says US 'obliterated' targets on Kharg Island
outlets.summary: IRGC threatens UAE cities. Macron calls for emergency UN session. Starmer says UK doesn't believe in 'regime change from the skies.'

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: IDF destroys Khamenei's aircraft at Mehrabad Airport
outlets.summary: Israeli strikes destroy Supreme Leader Khamenei's personal aircraft at Mehrabad Airport in Tehran. Satellite imagery confirms destroyed hangars on the military side of the airport. Symbolic blow to Iranian leadership.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: IDF begins wide-scale wave of strikes on western Iran
outlets.summary: IDF launches largest single-wave strike operation hitting 200+ targets across western Iran including IRGC headquarters, missile facilities, and command centers in Hamedan province.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Iran Intelligence Ministry Secretly Contacts CIA Through Third Country Backchannel
outlets.summary: Iran reaching out to CIA through intermediary intelligence service. Larijani and Araghchi using Oman as diplomatic conduit.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Iran fires 4 missile salvos at Israel; fragments fall near Church of Holy Sepulchre, Knesset
outlets.summary: Liveblog coverage: missile fragments near Jerusalem holy sites, IDF 400th strike wave milestone, home front restrictions extended through Wednesday.

outlets.name: Israel Hayom
outlets.headline: State Dept puts $10M bounty on Mojtaba Khamenei — US targets Iran's leadership directly
outlets.summary: $10M reward for information on Iran's Supreme Leader and 7 IRGC commanders. Netanyahu claims Israeli strikes killed nuclear scientists.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Iran fires 4 missile salvos at Israel; fragments fall near Church of Holy Sepulchre, Knesset
outlets.summary: Liveblog coverage: missile fragments near Jerusalem holy sites, IDF 400th strike wave milestone, home front restrictions extended through Wednesday.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Israel-Iran War Day 17 | Israel Strikes Across Iran; Red Cross: 900,000 Lebanese Displaced
outlets.summary: Day 17 liveblog: 900,000 displaced in Lebanon, Dubai airport drone strike, Saudi-Pakistan mutual defense pact activated.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Iran does not seek ceasefire but war must end, FM says
outlets.summary: Araghchi says Iran 'never asked for ceasefire' and reveals Russia-China military cooperation.

outlets.name: Tehran Times
outlets.headline: Araghchi Mocks Trump's 'Begging' for Naval Coalition — 'American Power Has Failed'
outlets.summary: FM Araghchi says Trump begging for warships shows US failure. IRGC maintains selective Hormuz blockade as leverage.

outlets.name: Iran International

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: 15,000+ targets hit by US — Iran vows to expand war to new fronts
outlets.summary: Frames war as Western aggression. Covers Mojtaba Khamenei's vow to keep Hormuz closed and demand US base closures. Larijani: war won't end until enemies are 'sorry.' Reports on yuan-denominated oil trade proposal.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Al-Quds Day rally explosion — Iranian people defiant despite US-Israeli bombing
outlets.summary: State agency reports Al-Quds Day rally explosion as evidence of enemy sabotage. Internet at 4% — state controls nearly all information flow. Reports on 2,200 Marines as escalation.
